name: Saint Mary's Cathedral of Kon Tum

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: The cathedral was built in the early of the 20 century by a French priest. The architecture is an interesting hybrid, Gothic style and Bahnar stilt houses, made of wood and painted black. Should come in the late morning or afternoon, when sunlight pass through the colorful glass windows, and the church becomes a kaleidoscope of colors, beautiful and impressive! One of must-visit in Kon Tum. Btw, there is an orphanage behind the church, visitors can come to have a look, support or donate.

reviews.author: Ross Galleymore
reviews.text: Beautiful, peaceful, relaxing space. The Church is quite impressive when you consider it's entirely wooden construction. Beautiful interior... most doors were locked and with no one inside we assumed it wasn't possible to enter... but a side door was wide open 🤩

reviews.author: Liễu Trần
reviews.text: Kon Tum Cathedral: A Must-See for Visitors to Vietnam's Central Highlands Kon Tum Cathedral, also known as the Kon Tum Wooden Church, is a beautiful and unique religious landmark located in the Central Highlands of Vietnam. The church is made entirely of wood, and its architecture is a blend of Romanesque and traditional Ba Na styles. The Kon Tum Cathedral was built in the early 20th century by French missionaries. The church is made of cà chít wood, a type of hardwood that is native to the region. The roof is made of thatch, and the interior is decorated with intricate carvings and paintings. The Kon Tum Cathedral is a popular tourist destination. Visitors can admire the church's beautiful architecture, learn about its history, and attend a religious service. Here are some tips for visiting the Kon Tum Cathedral: The church is located in the center of Kon Tum city. It is easily accessible by car, motorbike, or taxi. The church is open to visitors from 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM daily. There is no admission fee to visit the church. If you are planning a trip to Vietnam's Central Highlands, be sure to add the Kon Tum Cathedral to your itinerary. This beautiful and historic church is a must-see for any visitor to the region. Here are some specific things that British visitors might find interesting about the Kon Tum Cathedral: The church's unique blend of Romanesque and traditional Ba Na styles is a testament to the cultural diversity of Vietnam. The church's use of cà chít wood is a reminder of the region's rich natural resources. The church's intricate carvings and paintings are a testament to the skill and artistry of Vietnamese artisans.

reviews.author: jared lee
reviews.text: A very pleasant surprise to see this huge and majestic Cathedral, standing so strong despite the so many years of history. So well kept and maintained and a high Five to the Management and staff who upkeep this place!!! Love the magnificent architectural designs and unique structures from the exterior. I went inside the Cathedral and was amazed by the great beauty therein. Beautiful arches, colored windows and glasses... Love this place!!!!

reviews.author: Huyen Tran
reviews.text: Majestic church in Kon Tum! This 100 years old church was made from wood. The church design is a combination of Roman architecture and traditional stilt house style of Bahnar people. It is called a wooden church because the main material to build this church is wood abundant in the Central Highlands. All structures from columns, trusses, to floors are made of wood and glued together tenons without nails. The ceilings and walls are plastered with earthen materials mixed with straw in the style of the traditional houses of Central Vietnam. There is no reinforced concrete or mortar used. Must visit place in Kon Tum city!

reviews.author: Minh Ho
reviews.text: Must visit spot in Kon Tum city. This is 100 year old wooden cathedral. French built this by combining Roman catholic and local architecture. It took 6 years to complete and still stay intact. People use local instruments for prayers. Propably one of a kind. You can spend short time inside praying. Sunday is the best time to visit.

reviews.author: Khoa Do
reviews.text: This is a historical place with more than 100 years, you will be sure to have taken good picture with the nice architecture of the building and nice view. lucky when I was there, the sun was directly behind the church so I was able to take a lot of good picture, you will also learn about the history of the church through the people inside

reviews.author: lor cc
reviews.text: Visited this Catholic Church in Kon Tum during a solo trip to Pleiku. Lovely serene place. Was there a day before Easter.

reviews.author: Karolis
reviews.text: Curious wooden (not really) church. Nice colours, inside is pretty plain and ascetic, however some stain glass and statues inside. There is a nice yard to rest a bit with more statues, you can park inside. There is an orphanage behind the church.

reviews.author: Blair Orr
reviews.text: This is a lovely place which made me think of Vietnam's troubled colonial past. It was good to see devout people enjoy their visit. To reach their own. I especially liked seeing the local minority groups' faith represented outside.

reviews.author: Ania Natasza Górecka
reviews.text: The church is really adorable. It was nice to visit Christian Temple in the part of the world where buddishm is major religon. Nice thing is that the building is open so you can visit the interior too. The toilet however... Well let's say you better avoid it.

reviews.author: Constant Coolsma (Gh8strider)
reviews.text: Awesome (former) entirely wooden church built by the French. Wardamage is repaired with other materials than wood. Nice from the outside but really breathtaking from the inside I think, enjoy!

reviews.author: Trần Alex
reviews.text: Kon Tum wooden church is made of red-sen wood, ceiling and walls are built of soil mixed with straw. Designed according to Roman architecture, combined with the stilt house architecture of the indigenous Ba Na people from the texture lines to the accents on the material, it is bold cultural nuances and beliefs of the Central Highlands people. The church is a self-contained building consisting of a synagogue, a guest house, a house displaying ethnic and religious products, and a communal house.

reviews.author: Mightyman198x
reviews.text: Really nice church & must visit in Kontum. You shall have a free & peaceful time here. Enjoy

reviews.author: cuong dang
reviews.text: - A beautiful church should not miss as travel to Kontum.

reviews.author: Devon Nguyen
reviews.text: New renovation main cathedral at Kon Tum that made only by excellent wood and stained glass windows

reviews.author: Vu Duc Hau
reviews.text: A must visit place in Kontum province, special during Sunday, when the ethical people come in the morning for Sunday mass. The mass will be serviced by Bana language, the song also sing in Bana.

reviews.author: Alistair Urie
reviews.text: Local community was getting ready for Christmas. Regret that we couldn't stay until next week!

reviews.author: Richard jackson
reviews.text: A lovely old place, i spent time with the head Nun at the back in the living quarters for the orphans. It was good to see arable farm land being put to use. With a small amount of livestock,Sisters' most driving need was food, food to feed 180 mouths, average age 6yrs old live on donations and the grace of God. So please if you visit this grand Street front cathedral please leave a donation how ever small.

reviews.author: J V
reviews.text: Pretty unique church for Vietnam. Definitely worth a visit if you are nearby.

reviews.author: Jw
reviews.text: The old church is very beautiful, there are many trees outside, the air is fresh, the wedding photography here is also very beautiful.

reviews.author: Ho Ngoc Tin
reviews.text: Really impressive architecture design of both in and outside.

reviews.author: aibollit
reviews.text: A very beautiful place, the architect who designed it had good design taste

reviews.author: Thanh Tran
reviews.text: I feel relaxed, peaceful, and free from all worries and cares every time I’m there. Beautiful, special’s one in Kontum.

reviews.author: Tina Linh
reviews.text: Beautiful cathedral with unique architecture...should visit when arriving Kontum

reviews.author: Andrew
reviews.text: Glorious architecture and a tremendous sense of God's majesty and peace.

reviews.author: Nguyễn Thống Nhất
reviews.text: A must visit place for people who love antique buildings

reviews.author: Pradeep Menon
reviews.text: A beautiful Cathedral. Must visit if you come to Kon Tum
